title: Unable to see boards in a workspace
excerpt: I am an owner (blue crown) in an open workspace (no red pad lock). However I am still unable to see all the boards within that workspace. My collegue has access and the only way we have figured out how to fix this is to …

posts.author: tomhayes1994 (Tom Hayes)
posts.cookedHtml: I am an owner (blue crown) in an open workspace (no red pad lock). However I am still unable to see all the boards within that workspace. My collegue has access and the only way we have figured out how to fix this is to go to each board and manually change my permission. There are too many boards - is there an easy way that I can access all the boards at once?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
The other boards are private? Each board can be main (everyone in the account can see), private (the creator of the board has access to limit who can see) and shareable (everyone in the account can see & any guests that have the link can see).

The owner of those boards will need to either make the boards ‘main’ or add you to the boards individually.

posts.author: tomhayes1994 (Tom Hayes)
posts.cookedHtml:
Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador:

he creator of the board has access to limit who can see) and shareable (everyone in the account can see & any guests that have the link can see).

The owner of those boards will need to either make the boards ‘main’ or add you to the boards individually.

Thank you Desiree for your prompt reply. Changing the boards to ‘main’ seems to work however where would I go to, to allow my profile to view all the boards while keeping them private.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
You would have to be ‘invited’ to each private board individually using the invite button at top right of board.

image437×235 7.5 KB
